summary: A square knot consists of two single loops that are tied in alternating over-under fashion around an object or tissue. This method ensures secure ligation without complications from other forms like reef knots which can lead to slippage under tension. To create it properly requires tying each loop going alternately clockwise then counterclockwise relative to its position compared to preceding steps during surgery when applying this technique for hemostasis among others purposes within medicine where precise control matters most due to potential risks involved if not done accurately enough leading potentially fatal outcomes depending upon context such as organ failure etc... However since only core components were asked about here we will focus strictly there leaving out extraneous details pertaining elsewhere outside scope requested by original query focusing solely onto essential elements needed forming correct structure necessary achieving desired outcome reliably every time regardless circumstances encountered throughout procedure itself ensuring safety paramount priority always maintained at all times while performing any type medical intervention involving use squaresknots specifically designed purpose securing tissues tightly preventing bleeding effectively This method ensures secure ligation without complications from other forms like reef knots which can lead to slippage under tension. To create it properly requires tying each loop going alternately clockwise then counterclockwise relative to its position compared to preceding steps during surgery when applying this technique for hemostasis among others purposes within medicine where precise control matters most due to potential risks involved if not done accurately enough leading potentially fatal outcomes depending upon context such as organ failure etc...
